tasks.title: Fix TransactionBuilder cNFT Integration
tasks.description: Replace the placeholder error in transactionBuilder.ts with actual cNFT transfer instruction building using CnftService integration and Bubblegum program support.
tasks.details:
Implement comprehensive cNFT transfer support in the TransactionBuilder service with the following components:

1. **Replace Placeholder Implementation in buildAssetTransfers()**:
   - Remove the current "throw new Error('cNFT transfers not yet implemented')" placeholder
   - Add conditional logic to detect AssetType.CNFT vs AssetType.NFT in asset validation
   - Maintain existing standard NFT transfer logic for backward compatibility

2. **CnftService Integration**:
   - Import CnftService from '../services/cnftService'
   - Integrate CnftService.buildTransferParams() method to fetch Merkle proof data
   - Handle async proof fetching with proper error handling for network failures
   - Cache proof data temporarily during transaction building to avoid duplicate API calls

3. **Bubblegum Transfer Instruction Building**:
   - Import required types from @metaplex-foundation/mpl-bubblegum
   - Build transfer instruction using createTransferInstruction from mpl-bubblegum
   - Pass all required accounts: merkle_tree, tree_authority, bubblegum_program, compression_program, log_wrapper, noop_program
   - Derive tree_authority PDA using [merkle_tree.publicKey, "tree_authority"] seeds

4. **Merkle Proof Data Handling**:
   - Extract proof data from CnftService response (root, dataHash, creatorHash, nonce, leafIndex)
   - Handle canopy depth optimization by trimming proof array based on tree canopy depth
   - Implement proof validation before instruction creation to catch invalid proofs early
   - Add transaction size monitoring as proof arrays can significantly impact transaction size

5. **Account Management**:
   - Add all required Bubblegum program accounts to transaction
   - Handle both maker and taker cNFT transfers in same transaction when applicable
   - Ensure proper account ordering and signer requirements
   - Add remaining accounts for Merkle proof verification

6. **Error Handling and Validation**:
   - Add specific error types for cNFT-related failures (InvalidMerkleProof, StaleProof, TreeNotFound)
   - Implement retry logic for transient proof fetching failures
   - Validate tree authority matches expected PDA derivation
   - Check transaction size limits with proof data included

7. **Backward Compatibility**:
   - Ensure existing standard NFT transfer functionality remains unchanged
   - Support mixed swaps (standard NFT + cNFT in same transaction)
   - Maintain existing transaction structure and fee handling
tasks.testStrategy:
1. **Unit Testing**:
   - Test buildAssetTransfers() with cNFT assets and verify Bubblegum instruction creation
   - Mock CnftService.buildTransferParams() to test proof data integration
   - Test error handling for invalid Merkle proofs and network failures
   - Verify backward compatibility by testing standard NFT transfers still work
   - Test mixed asset transfers (standard NFT + cNFT combinations)

2. **Integration Testing**:
   - Test complete transaction building with real cNFT assets on devnet
   - Verify transaction size stays within 1232 byte limit with proof data
   - Test canopy depth optimization with different Merkle tree configurations
   - Validate all required accounts are properly included in instructions

3. **Error Scenario Testing**:
   - Test with stale Merkle proofs and verify proper error handling
   - Test with invalid tree authority PDAs and verify validation
   - Test network failures during proof fetching and verify retry logic
   - Test transaction size limit exceeded scenarios with large proof arrays

4. **Performance Testing**:
   - Measure transaction building time with proof fetching
   - Test concurrent proof fetching for multiple cNFTs
   - Verify proof caching reduces redundant API calls during transaction building

tasks.title: Remove 1-NFT-Per-Side Restriction in OfferManager for Bulk Swap Support
tasks.description: Update the OfferManager service to support bulk NFT swaps by removing the 1-NFT-per-side limitation and implementing support for up to 10 assets per side with proper validation and fee calculation.
tasks.details:
Implement bulk swap support in OfferManager with the following comprehensive updates:

1. **Remove Multi-Asset Restrictions**:
   - Update createOffer() method in src/services/offerManager.ts to remove existing 1-NFT-per-side validation
   - Replace single asset validation with array-based asset validation logic
   - Ensure backward compatibility with existing single-asset offers

2. **Implement New Asset Limits**:
   - Add MAX_ASSETS_PER_SIDE constant set to 10 for both offered and requested assets
   - Implement validation to reject offers exceeding the 10-asset limit per side
   - Add clear error messages for limit violations

3. **Enhanced Asset Validation**:
   - Update asset validation logic to handle arrays of multiple assets (NFTs, cNFTs, SOL)
   - Implement validation for mixed asset type combinations (cNFT + standard NFT + SOL)
   - Ensure each asset in the array passes individual validation (ownership, metadata, proof data for cNFTs)
   - Add duplicate asset detection within the same offer side

4. **Bulk Fee Calculation Integration**:
   - Update fee calculation calls to handle multiple assets by summing individual asset values
   - Integrate with existing FeeCalculator service to process bulk asset arrays
   - Ensure proper fee calculation for mixed asset types (flat fees for NFTs, percentage for SOL)

5. **Database Storage Optimization**:
   - Verify SwapOffer.offeredAssets and SwapOffer.requestedAssets Json fields handle bulk arrays
   - Update offer serialization/deserialization to properly handle multi-asset structures
   - Ensure database queries efficiently handle bulk asset data

6. **Offer Management Updates**:
   - Update offer listing methods to display multi-asset offers with proper asset summaries
   - Modify offer retrieval to handle bulk asset metadata and display information
   - Update offer status tracking to account for multi-asset complexity

7. **Comprehensive Validation Rules**:
   - Implement validation for valid bulk swap combinations (ensure at least one asset per side)
   - Add business logic validation for reasonable swap combinations
   - Ensure total transaction size considerations for bulk swaps

tasks.title: Phase 2b - Bulk Swap Support: Implement Transaction Splitting for Bulk cNFT Swaps
tasks.description: Implement intelligent transaction splitting for bulk cNFT swaps where each cNFT transfer requires ~7+ additional accounts, splitting into 1-2 cNFTs per transaction with Address Lookup Tables and Jito bundle atomicity.
tasks.details:
Implement the TransactionGroupBuilder class and bulk swap transaction splitting with the following comprehensive components:

1. **TransactionGroupBuilder Class Creation**:
   - Create new TransactionGroupBuilder class in src/services/transactionBuilder.ts
   - Extend existing TransactionBuilder functionality to handle multi-transaction groups
   - Implement transaction size estimation logic with 1,232 byte limit per transaction
   - Add account counting for cNFT transfers (~7+ accounts per cNFT: merkle_tree, tree_authority, bubblegum_program, compression_program, log_wrapper, system_program, proof accounts)

2. **Conservative Strategy Selection Logic**:
   - Implement asset count analysis with conservative 1-2 cNFTs per transaction maximum
   - Create threshold logic: 1-2 total cNFTs → single transaction (no bundle), 3+ total cNFTs → multi-transaction with Jito bundle
   - Conservative approach ensures staying well under 1,232-byte limit even with full merkle proofs
   - Add fallback logic for edge cases where even single cNFT exceeds transaction limits
   - Future optimization path to 3-5 cNFTs per transaction after canopy depth testing

3. **Transaction Splitting Implementation**:
   - Split bulk swaps into groups of 1-2 cNFTs per transaction (conservative approach)
   - Implement smart ordering: payments first → NFT transfers → SOL cleanup last
   - Ensure proper nonce account assignment for each transaction in the group
   - Maintain transaction dependencies and execution order
   - Only use Jito bundles for swaps with 3+ total cNFTs

4. **Address Lookup Tables (ALTs) Integration**:
   - Create ALTs for common accounts (bubblegum_program, compression_program, system_program, log_wrapper)
   - Implement ALT creation and management for frequently used program accounts
   - Use Versioned Transactions with ALT support to reduce transaction size
   - Add ALT caching and reuse logic for efficiency

5. **Conditional Jito Bundle Atomicity**:
   - Use Jito bundles only for swaps with 3+ total cNFTs requiring transaction splitting
   - Implement bundle creation with proper transaction ordering for multi-transaction swaps
   - Add bundle status tracking and confirmation logic
   - Handle bundle failure scenarios with proper error reporting
   - Skip bundle overhead for simple 1-2 cNFT swaps using single transactions

6. **Database Schema Extensions**:
   - Extend SwapOffer model or create new TransactionGroup model
   - Add transactionGroup and bundleId fields for tracking multi-transaction swaps
   - Implement transaction group status tracking (pending, submitted, confirmed, failed)
   - Add partial execution tracking for rollback scenarios

7. **Partial Failure Handling**:
   - Implement transaction group rollback logic for partial failures
   - Add compensation transaction creation for successful partial executions
   - Create detailed failure reporting with specific transaction failure reasons
   - Implement retry logic for transient failures

tasks.title: Implement Jito Bundle Submission for Bulk Swap Parallel Execution
tasks.description: Extend the existing Jito integration to support bundle submission for bulk swaps with 3+ total cNFTs, enabling atomic parallel transaction execution with proper ordering, simulation, and confirmation handling.
tasks.details:
Implement comprehensive Jito bundle submission functionality in src/services/escrow-program.service.ts with the following components:

1. **Extend Existing Jito Integration**:
   - Build upon existing sendTransactionViaJito() method documented in docs/JITO_FREE_INTEGRATION_SUMMARY.md
   - Maintain compatibility with current single transaction Jito submissions
   - Reuse existing Jito endpoint configuration and authentication setup

2. **Bundle Creation and Management**:
   - Implement sendBundleViaJito() method that accepts array of Transaction objects
   - Create Jito bundle with proper transaction ordering (nonce advance first, transfers, fee collection last)
   - Implement bundle size validation (max 5 transactions per bundle as per Jito limits)
   - Add bundle threshold logic: use bundles for swaps with 3+ total cNFTs (since 1-2 cNFTs fit in single transaction, but 3+ require multiple transactions due to account limits), otherwise use single transaction

3. **Dynamic Tip Calculation**:
   - Implement dynamic Jito tip calculation based on current network congestion
   - Query Jito tip stream API for recommended tip amounts
   - Include tip as final instruction in last transaction of bundle
   - Add fallback tip amounts for API failures (0.001 SOL default)

4. **Bundle Simulation and Validation**:
   - Implement bundle simulation using Jito's simulateBundle endpoint before submission
   - Validate all transactions in bundle pass simulation
   - Handle simulation failures with detailed error reporting
   - Add retry logic with fresh Merkle proofs on simulation failures

5. **Bundle Submission and Confirmation**:
   - Submit bundle via Jito's sendBundle endpoint
   - Implement bundle ID tracking in database with new bundle_id column in swap_transactions table
   - Use getBundleStatuses endpoint for confirmation polling
   - Handle bundle landing rate monitoring and timeout scenarios (30 second timeout)

6. **Error Handling and Retry Logic**:
   - Implement exponential backoff retry for bundle failures
   - Refresh Merkle proofs on retry attempts for cNFT transactions
   - Handle partial bundle confirmation scenarios
   - Add fallback to individual transaction submission on repeated bundle failures

7. **Comprehensive Logging**:
   - Log complete bundle lifecycle: creation, simulation, submission, confirmation
   - Include bundle ID, transaction count, tip amount, and timing metrics
   - Add structured logging for bundle performance analytics
   - Implement error categorization for bundle failure analysis

8. **Integration with Existing Services**:
   - Update TransactionBuilder service to flag transactions suitable for bundling
   - Modify OfferManager to trigger bundle submission for qualifying bulk swaps
   - Ensure compatibility with existing nonce pool management and fee calculation

subtasks.details: Modify the existing sendTransactionViaJito() method in src/services/escrow-program.service.ts to add bundle support parameters. Add optional bundleMode flag and bundleTransactions array parameter. Maintain existing single transaction flow as default behavior. Reuse existing Jito endpoint configuration and authentication setup from docs/JITO_FREE_INTEGRATION_SUMMARY.md. Ensure no breaking changes to current single transaction Jito submissions.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test single transaction submissions still work unchanged. Test new bundle parameters are properly handled. Verify existing Jito configuration is reused correctly.

subtasks.title: Implement sendBundleViaJito() Method with Transaction Ordering
subtasks.description: Create new sendBundleViaJito() method that accepts array of Transaction objects and creates properly ordered Jito bundles.
subtasks.details: Implement sendBundleViaJito() method that accepts Transaction[] parameter. Create Jito bundle with proper transaction ordering: nonce advance transactions first, transfer transactions in middle, fee collection transactions last. Implement bundle size validation with maximum 5 transactions per bundle as per Jito limits. Add bundle threshold logic to use bundles for swaps with 3+ total cNFTs (since 1-2 cNFTs fit in single transaction, but 3+ require multiple transactions due to account limits), otherwise fallback to single transaction submission.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test bundle creation with 2-5 transactions. Verify proper transaction ordering in bundles. Test bundle threshold logic with exactly 3 cNFTs vs 2 cNFTs. Validate bundle size limits are enforced.

subtasks.title: Implement Dynamic Tip Calculation with Jito API Integration
subtasks.description: Implement dynamic Jito tip calculation based on current network congestion using Jito tip stream API.
subtasks.details: Query Jito tip stream API for recommended tip amounts based on current network congestion. Implement dynamic tip calculation logic that adjusts based on network conditions. Include tip as final instruction in last transaction of bundle. Add fallback tip amounts for API failures with 0.001 SOL default. Create tip calculation helper functions with proper error handling and timeout logic.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test tip API integration with various network conditions. Verify tip is added as final instruction in last transaction. Test fallback behavior when tip API fails. Validate tip amounts are reasonable and within expected ranges.

subtasks.title: Implement Bundle Simulation and Validation System
subtasks.description: Implement comprehensive bundle simulation using Jito's simulateBundle endpoint with validation and retry logic.
subtasks.details: Implement bundle simulation using Jito's simulateBundle endpoint before submission. Validate all transactions in bundle pass simulation with detailed error reporting. Handle simulation failures with proper error categorization and logging. Add retry logic with fresh Merkle proofs on simulation failures for cNFT transactions. Implement simulation timeout handling and fallback strategies.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test bundle simulation with valid and invalid transaction bundles. Verify simulation failures are properly detected and reported. Test retry logic with fresh Merkle proofs. Validate simulation timeout handling works correctly.

subtasks.title: Implement Bundle Submission and Confirmation Tracking
subtasks.description: Implement bundle submission via Jito's sendBundle endpoint with database tracking and confirmation polling.
subtasks.details: Submit bundle via Jito's sendBundle endpoint with proper error handling. Implement bundle ID tracking in database by adding bundle_id column to swap_transactions table. Use getBundleStatuses endpoint for confirmation polling with 30 second timeout. Handle bundle landing rate monitoring and timeout scenarios. Implement confirmation status updates in database with proper transaction state management.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test bundle submission with various bundle sizes. Verify bundle ID is properly stored in database. Test confirmation polling with successful and failed bundles. Validate timeout handling and status updates work correctly.

subtasks.title: Implement Comprehensive Error Handling and Retry Logic
subtasks.description: Implement robust error handling, retry logic, and comprehensive logging for the complete bundle submission lifecycle.
subtasks.details: Implement exponential backoff retry for bundle failures with configurable retry limits. Refresh Merkle proofs on retry attempts for cNFT transactions. Handle partial bundle confirmation scenarios with proper cleanup. Add fallback to individual transaction submission on repeated bundle failures. Implement comprehensive logging for complete bundle lifecycle including creation, simulation, submission, and confirmation. Add structured logging with bundle ID, transaction count, tip amount, and timing metrics. Include error categorization for bundle failure analysis and performance analytics.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test retry logic with various failure scenarios. Verify Merkle proof refresh on retries. Test fallback to individual transactions after bundle failures. Validate comprehensive logging captures all bundle lifecycle events and metrics.

tasks.title: Enhance Private Sales with Taker Wallet Restriction
tasks.description: Enhance the private sales functionality by allowing makers to optionally specify a taker wallet address, restricting who can accept the offer.
tasks.details:
Implement comprehensive private sales functionality with taker wallet restrictions:

1. **Verify Database Schema**:
   - Confirm SwapOffer.takerWallet field exists in Prisma schema as String? (optional field)
   - Ensure proper database migration if field modifications are needed

2. **Update Offer Creation Logic**:
   - Modify createOffer() function in src/services/offerManager.ts to accept optional takerWallet parameter
   - Add Solana public key validation using @solana/web3.js PublicKey constructor with try-catch for invalid keys
   - Store takerWallet in database when provided, null for public offers

3. **Implement Taker Wallet Enforcement**:
   - Update acceptOffer() function to check if offer has takerWallet restriction
   - Compare requesting taker's wallet address with stored takerWallet using PublicKey.equals()
   - Return HTTP 403 with clear error message "This is a private offer restricted to a specific wallet" for unauthorized attempts

4. **API Enhancements**:
   - Update offer listing endpoints in src/routes/offers.routes.ts to include isPrivate boolean field (true when takerWallet is not null)
   - Add "my-private-offers" endpoint filtering offers where takerWallet matches authenticated user's wallet
   - Implement query parameter filtering for public vs private offers in general listing

5. **API Documentation Updates**:
   - Update OpenAPI specification to document new takerWallet parameter in createOffer endpoint
   - Document new isPrivate field in offer response schemas
   - Add examples showing private offer creation and restriction behavior
tasks.testStrategy:
1. **Database and Validation Testing**:
   - Verify SwapOffer.takerWallet field accepts valid Solana public keys and null values
   - Test createOffer() with valid public keys, invalid keys (should reject), and null values
   - Confirm database stores takerWallet correctly for both private and public offers

2. **Private Offer Enforcement Testing**:
   - Create private offer with specific taker wallet, attempt acceptance from authorized wallet (should succeed)
   - Attempt acceptance from unauthorized wallet (should return 403 error with appropriate message)
   - Test public offers (takerWallet=null) can be accepted by any wallet

3. **API Endpoint Testing**:
   - Test offer listing includes correct isPrivate field values
   - Verify "my-private-offers" endpoint returns only offers where user's wallet matches takerWallet
   - Test filtering parameters for public vs private offers work correctly

4. **Integration Testing**:
   - End-to-end test creating private offer, listing it, and completing swap with authorized taker
   - Verify private offers don't appear in general public listings unless specifically requested
   - Test API documentation examples work as documented

tasks.title: Phase 3b - Enhanced Offer Management: Implement Counter-Offer Functionality
tasks.description: Implement comprehensive counter-offer functionality allowing users to submit counter-offers for existing swap offers with proper validation, chain tracking, and workflow management.
tasks.details:
Implement the counter-offer functionality with the following comprehensive components:

1. **Database Schema Verification**:
   - Verify SwapOffer.parentOfferId field exists as Int? in prisma/schema.prisma for offer chain tracking
   - Ensure proper foreign key relationship to parent SwapOffer records
   - Confirm database supports recursive offer chain queries

2. **Counter-Offer Creation Logic**:
   - Implement submitCounterOffer() method in src/services/offerManager.ts
   - Accept parameters: originalOfferId, counterOfferAssets, makerWallet, expirationTime
   - Validate counter-offer assets against original offer context (ensure reasonable asset types)
   - Link counter-offers to parent using parentOfferId field
   - Generate unique counter-offer ID and set appropriate status (PENDING)

3. **Offer Chain Management**:
   - Implement getOfferChain() method to traverse counter-offer chains using parentOfferId
   - Create recursive query logic to fetch complete offer history from root to latest counter-offer
   - Add chain depth validation to prevent excessive counter-offer nesting (max 10 levels)
   - Implement chain status tracking to identify active vs expired offers in chain

4. **Original Offer Status Updates**:
   - Update original offer status to COUNTER_OFFERED when counter-offer is submitted
   - Implement status transition validation (only ACTIVE offers can receive counter-offers)
   - Add timestamp tracking for counter-offer submission events
   - Ensure original offer remains accessible for reference but marked as superseded

5. **API Endpoint Implementation**:
   - Create POST /api/offers/:id/counter-offer endpoint in src/routes/offers.routes.ts
   - Implement request validation for counter-offer parameters
   - Add authentication middleware to verify maker wallet ownership
   - Return complete counter-offer details including chain position and parent reference

6. **Counter-Offer Workflow Management**:
   - Implement acceptCounterOffer() and rejectCounterOffer() methods
   - Handle counter-offer acceptance by creating atomic swap transaction
   - Manage counter-offer rejection with proper status updates and chain cleanup
   - Add expiration handling for counter-offers with automatic status updates

7. **Notification Hooks Preparation**:
   - Add event emission points for counter-offer submission, acceptance, and rejection
   - Implement structured event data format for future notification system integration
   - Create placeholder hooks in offerManager.ts for notification service calls

8. **API Documentation Updates**:
   - Document counter-offer flow in API documentation
   - Include request/response schemas for counter-offer endpoints
   - Add examples showing complete counter-offer chain interactions
   - Document error codes and validation rules for counter-offer operations
tasks.testStrategy:
1. **Counter-Offer Creation Testing**:
   - Test submitCounterOffer() with valid original offers and verify counter-offer creation with correct parentOfferId linking
   - Validate counter-offer asset validation against original offer context (reject incompatible asset types)
   - Test counter-offer creation with invalid original offer IDs and verify proper error handling
   - Verify original offer status updates to COUNTER_OFFERED when counter-offer is submitted

2. **Offer Chain Functionality Testing**:
   - Test getOfferChain() with single offers, simple chains (2-3 levels), and complex chains (5+ levels)
   - Verify recursive chain traversal returns complete offer history in correct chronological order
   - Test chain depth validation by attempting to create 11+ level chains and verify rejection
   - Test chain queries with mixed offer statuses (active, expired, accepted, rejected)

3. **API Endpoint Testing**:
   - Test POST /api/offers/:id/counter-offer with valid authentication and offer data, verify 201 response with complete counter-offer details
   - Test endpoint with invalid offer IDs, expired offers, and already-accepted offers, verify appropriate 400/404 error responses
   - Test authentication middleware by attempting counter-offer creation without proper wallet verification
   - Verify API response includes parentOfferId, chain position, and complete asset details

4. **Counter-Offer Workflow Testing**:
   - Test acceptCounterOffer() and rejectCounterOffer() methods with various chain positions and verify proper status transitions
   - Test counter-offer acceptance creates atomic swap transaction using existing TransactionBuilder service
   - Test counter-offer expiration handling with automated status updates and chain cleanup
   - Verify notification event emission at all workflow transition points

5. **Integration Testing**:
   - Test complete counter-offer flow from creation through acceptance/rejection with real database operations
   - Verify counter-offer functionality integrates properly with existing offer management and swap execution systems
   - Test concurrent counter-offer operations on same original offer and verify proper conflict resolution
   - Test counter-offer chain queries under load with multiple simultaneous chain traversals

tasks.title: Phase 3c - Enhanced Offer Management: Implement Cancel Swap Functionality
tasks.description: Implement comprehensive cancel swap functionality allowing makers and admins to cancel pending swap offers with proper validation, database updates, and resource cleanup.
tasks.details:
Implement the cancel swap functionality with the following comprehensive components:

1. **Database Schema Updates**:
   - Add cancelledAt DateTime? and cancelledBy String? fields to SwapOffer model in Prisma schema
   - Create and run database migration to add new fields
   - Update TypeScript types to reflect schema changes

2. **Core Cancellation Logic in offerManager.ts**:
   - Implement cancelOffer(offerId: string, cancelledBy: string, isAdmin: boolean) method
   - Add validation to ensure only offers in 'open' or 'pending' status can be cancelled
   - Implement permission checks: maker wallet address or admin authorization required
   - Update offer status to 'cancelled' and set cancelledAt timestamp and cancelledBy fields
   - Release any reserved nonce accounts associated with the offer back to the pool
   - Handle cleanup of any pending transactions or escrow state

3. **API Endpoint Implementation**:
   - Create DELETE /api/offers/:id endpoint in src/routes/offers.routes.ts
   - Alternative POST /api/offers/:id/cancel endpoint for better REST semantics
   - Implement request validation middleware for offer ID format and existence
   - Add authentication middleware to verify maker wallet signature or admin token
   - Return appropriate HTTP status codes (200 for success, 403 for unauthorized, 404 for not found, 400 for invalid status)

4. **Authorization and Security**:
   - Implement maker wallet verification by comparing request signature with offer.makerWallet
   - Add admin authorization check using existing admin authentication system
   - Validate that cancellation requester matches either maker wallet or has admin privileges
   - Add rate limiting to prevent abuse of cancellation endpoints

5. **Resource Cleanup and State Management**:
   - Release nonce accounts back to NoncePoolManager if they were reserved for the offer
   - Clean up any temporary escrow accounts or pending transaction state
   - Update related database records and maintain referential integrity
   - Log cancellation events for audit trail and monitoring

6. **Error Handling and Logging**:
   - Implement comprehensive error handling for database failures, RPC errors, and validation failures
   - Add structured logging for all cancellation attempts (successful and failed)
   - Create specific error messages for different failure scenarios
   - Ensure atomic operations where possible to prevent partial state updates
tasks.testStrategy:
1. **Core Functionality Testing**:
   - Test cancelOffer() method with valid offers in 'open' and 'pending' status, verify status updates to 'cancelled'
   - Test cancellation rejection for offers in 'completed', 'expired', or already 'cancelled' status
   - Verify cancelledAt timestamp and cancelledBy fields are properly set in database
   - Test nonce account release by checking NoncePoolManager pool size before and after cancellation

2. **Authorization Testing**:
   - Test maker wallet cancellation by creating offer with test wallet and cancelling with same wallet signature
   - Test admin cancellation with valid admin token and verify admin bypass of maker wallet check
   - Test unauthorized cancellation attempts (wrong wallet, no admin token) and verify 403 responses
   - Verify cancellation fails when neither maker wallet nor admin authorization is provided

3. **API Endpoint Testing**:
   - Test DELETE /api/offers/:id with valid offer IDs and proper authentication
   - Test POST /api/offers/:id/cancel alternative endpoint functionality
   - Verify proper HTTP status codes for various scenarios (200, 403, 404, 400)
   - Test malformed offer IDs and non-existent offers return appropriate errors

4. **Edge Cases and Error Handling**:
   - Test cancellation of offers with associated nonce accounts and verify proper cleanup
   - Test database connection failures during cancellation and verify rollback behavior
   - Test concurrent cancellation attempts on same offer and verify only one succeeds
   - Verify cancellation works correctly for both regular and bulk swap offers

5. **Integration Testing**:
   - Test end-to-end cancellation flow from API request through database update and resource cleanup
   - Verify cancelled offers no longer appear in active offer listings
   - Test that cancelled offers cannot be accepted or modified further
   - Confirm audit logging captures all cancellation events with proper details

tasks.title: Phase 3d - Enhanced Offer Management: Implement Update Listing Functionality
tasks.description: Implement comprehensive functionality for makers to update their existing swap listings, allowing modifications to SOL amounts and swap assets while maintaining proper validation and version tracking.
tasks.details:
Implement the complete update listing functionality with the following comprehensive components:

1. **Database Schema Updates**:
   - Add previousVersionId Int? field to SwapOffer model for version tracking
   - Add updateCount Int default 0 field to track number of updates
   - Create database migration to add these fields to existing offers table
   - Ensure proper indexing on previousVersionId for efficient version queries

2. **Core updateOffer() Method Implementation**:
   - Create updateOffer(offerId, updateData, makerWallet) method in src/services/offerManager.ts
   - Validate offer exists and is in 'open' status (reject 'accepted', 'completed', 'cancelled' offers)
   - Verify maker wallet authorization matches original offer creator
   - Support modification of: SOL amounts (makerSolAmount, takerSolAmount), offered assets array, requested assets array
   - Increment updateCount and set previousVersionId to current offer version

3. **Asset Re-validation and Fee Recalculation**:
   - Re-validate all updated assets using existing asset validation logic
   - Verify NFT/cNFT ownership for newly added offered assets
   - Recalculate platform fees using FeeCalculator service based on new asset values
   - Update offer totals and fee breakdowns in database

4. **Transaction Invalidation Handling**:
   - Check for any pending transactions associated with the offer
   - Invalidate existing prepared transactions that reference old offer data
   - Clear any cached transaction builders or nonce assignments
   - Log transaction invalidations for monitoring purposes

5. **API Endpoint Implementation**:
   - Create PUT /api/offers/:id endpoint in src/routes/offers.routes.ts
   - Add request validation middleware for update payload structure
   - Implement proper error handling for unauthorized updates, invalid status, and validation failures
   - Return updated offer data with new version information

6. **Update Cooldown and Spam Prevention**:
   - Implement configurable update cooldown period (e.g., 5 minutes between updates)
   - Store lastUpdatedAt timestamp and validate against cooldown period
   - Add rate limiting specifically for offer updates per wallet address
   - Log excessive update attempts for monitoring

7. **Version History and Audit Trail**:
   - Maintain complete audit trail of offer modifications
   - Store previous offer state before applying updates
   - Enable version history queries for debugging and analytics
   - Implement soft deletion approach for version tracking

8. **API Documentation Updates**:
   - Document PUT /api/offers/:id endpoint with request/response schemas
   - Include error response codes and validation requirements
   - Add examples for different update scenarios (SOL only, assets only, combined)
   - Document version tracking and cooldown behavior
tasks.testStrategy:
1. **Core Functionality Testing**:
   - Test updateOffer() method with valid updates to SOL amounts, offered assets, and requested assets
   - Verify only 'open' status offers can be updated (test rejection of 'accepted', 'completed', 'cancelled' offers)
   - Test maker wallet authorization by attempting updates with wrong wallet addresses
   - Verify updateCount increments correctly and previousVersionId is set properly

2. **Asset Validation and Fee Testing**:
   - Test asset re-validation with newly added NFTs/cNFTs (verify ownership checks)
   - Test removal of assets from offers and ensure proper validation
   - Verify fee recalculation accuracy using FeeCalculator service with updated asset values
   - Test edge cases like removing all SOL amounts or all assets

3. **API Endpoint Testing**:
   - Test PUT /api/offers/:id with valid update payloads and verify 200 responses
   - Test unauthorized update attempts and verify 403 responses
   - Test updates on non-existent offers and verify 404 responses
   - Test updates on offers with invalid status and verify 400 responses with proper error messages

4. **Update Cooldown and Rate Limiting Testing**:
   - Test update cooldown by making rapid successive updates and verify rejection after cooldown period
   - Test rate limiting by exceeding update limits per wallet and verify 429 responses
   - Verify cooldown resets properly after waiting period expires

5. **Transaction Invalidation Testing**:
   - Create offer with pending prepared transaction, then update offer and verify transaction is invalidated
   - Test that updated offers require new transaction preparation
   - Verify nonce assignments are cleared when offers are updated

6. **Version History and Database Testing**:
   - Verify database schema changes are applied correctly with migration
   - Test version tracking by making multiple updates and querying version history
   - Test previousVersionId linking and updateCount accuracy across multiple updates
   - Verify database constraints and indexing work properly for version fields

7. **Integration Testing**:
   - Test complete update flow from API request through database update and response
   - Verify updated offers appear correctly in listing queries
   - Test interaction with existing offer management functionality (creation, acceptance, cancellation)
   - Test update functionality with various offer types (NFT-to-NFT, NFT-to-SOL, bulk swaps)

tasks.title: Database Schema Updates for Bulk cNFT Swaps

tasks.details:

   - Add `bundleId: String?` for Jito bundle ID storage
   - Add `previousVersionId: Int?` for offer update versioning and history tracking
   - Add `updateCount: Int @default(0)` to track number of offer modifications
   - Add `cancelledAt: DateTime?` to timestamp offer cancellations
   - Add `cancelledBy: String?` to track which wallet cancelled the offer

2. **Create Optional TransactionGroup Model**:
   - Evaluate need for separate TransactionGroup model for complex bundle tracking

   - Add relation to SwapOffer if TransactionGroup model is created

   - Verify new fields are properly typed in generated client
   - Update any existing TypeScript interfaces that reference SwapOffer

5. **Database Operations Testing**:
   - Test CRUD operations with new optional fields
   - Verify default values work correctly (updateCount: 0)
   - Test null handling for optional fields
   - Validate foreign key constraints if TransactionGroup model is added

6. **Seed Data Updates**:

   - Test migration rollback if supported

3. **Database Operations Testing**:

   - Test updateCount increments correctly when offers are modified
   - Verify cancelledAt and cancelledBy fields work for offer cancellation tracking

4. **Integration Testing**:

   - Test that existing API endpoints still work with updated schema

   - Test Prisma Client queries with new fields in development environment

5. **Production Readiness**:

   - Verify migration can be applied to production without data loss

tasks.title: Implement DAS API Rate Limiting and Batched Proof Fetching
tasks.description: Implement rate limiting, batching, and caching for DAS API proof fetching to handle bulk cNFT swaps with 10+ assets without hitting API rate limits.
tasks.details:
Implement comprehensive rate limiting and batching for DAS API proof fetching with the following components:

1. **Rate Limiting Infrastructure in CnftService**:
   - Add rate limiter using p-limit library with configurable concurrent request limit (default 5)
   - Implement request queue with priority handling for proof fetching operations
   - Add configurable delay between batched requests (200-500ms) to respect API limits
   - Create RateLimitConfig interface with maxConcurrent, batchSize, and delayMs properties

2. **Batched Proof Fetching Implementation**:
   - Implement batchedGetCnftProofs(assetIds: string[], batchSize: number = 3) method
   - Process proof requests in groups of 3-5 with configurable delays between batches
   - Use Promise.allSettled() for concurrent processing within each batch
   - Add proper error handling for individual proof fetch failures without failing entire batch

tasks.title: Implement Proof Freshness Strategy for Bulk cNFT Swaps
tasks.description: Implement comprehensive proof freshness management for bulk cNFT swaps to prevent stale Merkle proof errors through just-in-time fetching, validation, and automatic refresh mechanisms.
tasks.details:
Implement comprehensive proof freshness strategy for bulk cNFT swaps with the following components:

1. **Just-in-Time Proof Fetching in CnftService**:
   - Modify getCnftProof() method to accept optional fetchTime parameter for tracking
   - Implement fetchProofJIT() method that fetches proofs immediately before transaction building
   - Add proof caching with 30-second TTL using Map<string, {proof: any, timestamp: number}>
   - Create ProofFreshnessConfig interface with expirySeconds, maxRetries, refreshThreshold

2. **Proof Validation Before Transaction Building**:
   - Add validateProofFreshness() method in CnftService to check root matches on-chain
   - Implement getCurrentTreeRoot() using Bubblegum program account fetching
   - Add proof validation step in TransactionBuilder.buildAssetTransfers() before instruction creation
   - Create ProofValidationResult interface with isValid, currentRoot, proofRoot, staleness flags

3. **Automatic Proof Refresh on Stale Errors**:
   - Implement refreshStaleProof() method with exponential backoff retry logic
   - Add error detection for "InvalidProof" and "TreeRootMismatch" transaction errors
   - Create proof refresh queue with priority handling for failed transactions
   - Add ProofRefreshStrategy enum (IMMEDIATE, BATCHED, SCHEDULED)

4. **Proof Expiry Timestamp Tracking**:
   - Add proofTimestamp and expiresAt fields to proof cache entries
   - Implement isProofStale() utility function checking 30-second expiry window
   - Create proof cleanup job to remove expired entries from cache
   - Add ProofMetadata interface with fetchTime, treeRoot, canopyDepth, expiresAt

5. **Rebuild-Transaction Endpoint**:
   - Create POST /api/offers/:id/rebuild-transaction endpoint in offers.routes.ts
   - Implement rebuildFailedTransaction() method in offerManager.ts
   - Add transaction failure reason detection and appropriate rebuild strategy
   - Support both single transaction and bundle transaction rebuilding

6. **Concurrent Tree Modification Detection**:
   - Implement detectConcurrentModifications() using tree sequence number tracking
   - Add tree state monitoring during bulk swap execution
   - Create TreeStateMonitor class with real-time change detection
   - Implement transaction sequencing to minimize proof staleness window

7. **Transaction Sequencing Optimization**:
   - Modify TransactionGroupBuilder to order transactions by proof freshness priority
   - Implement proof-aware transaction batching with freshness scoring
   - Add sequential execution mode for high-value swaps requiring maximum freshness
   - Create ProofFreshnessScore calculation based on tree activity and proof age

8. **Architecture Documentation**:
   - Document proof freshness strategy in docs/PROOF_FRESHNESS_STRATEGY.md
   - Include proof lifecycle diagrams and freshness decision trees
   - Add troubleshooting guide for common proof staleness scenarios
   - Document configuration options and performance tuning recommendations

tasks.title: Implement Bundle Failure Recovery and Monitoring
tasks.description: Implement comprehensive bundle failure recovery procedures and monitoring system to track Jito bundle health, handle partial executions, and provide automated retry mechanisms with proper alerting.
tasks.details:
Implement comprehensive bundle failure recovery and monitoring system with the following components:

1. **Bundle Status Tracking Database Schema**:
   - Add BundleStatus enum (PENDING, SUBMITTED, LANDED, FAILED, PARTIAL) to Prisma schema
   - Add bundleStatus field to SwapOffer model with default PENDING
   - Add bundleSubmittedAt, bundleLandedAt, bundleFailedAt DateTime fields
   - Add bundleRetryCount Int @default(0) and maxRetries Int @default(3)
   - Add bundleFailureReason String? for error tracking
   - Create database migration for new fields

2. **Enhanced Bundle Status Tracking in EscrowProgramService**:
   - Extend sendBundleViaJito() method to update bundle status throughout lifecycle
   - Implement updateBundleStatus(offerId: string, status: BundleStatus, metadata?: any) method
   - Add bundle confirmation polling with exponential backoff (5s, 10s, 20s, 40s intervals)
   - Track bundle landing time and update bundleLandedAt timestamp
   - Store Jito bundle signatures and transaction hashes for recovery

3. **Bundle Recovery Script Implementation**:
   - Create src/scripts/bundleRecovery.ts for partial bundle failure analysis
   - Implement analyzePartialBundle(bundleId: string) to identify successful/failed transactions
   - Add recoverPartialBundle() method to retry only failed transactions with fresh proofs
   - Integrate with proof freshness strategy from Task 59 for retry attempts
   - Create manual recovery CLI interface for admin operations

4. **Automatic Retry Mechanism**:
   - Implement retryFailedBundle(offerId: string) method in EscrowProgramService
   - Add exponential backoff retry logic (1min, 5min, 15min delays)
   - Fetch fresh Merkle proofs before each retry attempt using Task 59 proof freshness
   - Update retry count and prevent infinite retries (max 3 attempts)
   - Mark bundles as permanently failed after max retries exceeded

5. **Monitoring Service Implementation**:
   - Create src/services/monitoring.service.ts with comprehensive bundle metrics
   - Implement collectBundleMetrics() method tracking success/failure rates over time windows
   - Add calculateAverageLandingTime() for bundle performance monitoring
   - Track tip amounts and transactions per bundle for optimization insights
   - Store metrics in time-series format for historical analysis
   - Create getBundleHealthReport() method for admin dashboard

6. **Alerting Service Integration**:
   - Create src/services/alerting.service.ts for bundle failure notifications
   - Implement checkBundleFailureRate() with configurable thresholds (>20% failure rate)
   - Add Slack/Discord webhook integration for high-priority alerts
   - Create email notifications for bundle failure spikes
   - Implement alert throttling to prevent notification spam

7. **Frontend Status Polling Endpoint**:
   - Add GET /api/bundles/:offerId/status endpoint for real-time status updates
   - Implement WebSocket connection for live bundle status streaming
   - Return detailed bundle information including retry attempts and failure reasons
   - Add pagination for bundle history and status logs

8. **Admin Management Endpoints**:
   - Create POST /api/admin/bundles/:offerId/retry for manual bundle retry
   - Add POST /api/admin/bundles/:offerId/cancel for bundle cancellation
   - Implement GET /api/admin/bundles/failed for failed bundle dashboard
   - Add bulk operations for managing multiple failed bundles

9. **Documentation Updates**:
   - Update docs/BULK_CNFT_SWAP_ARCHITECTURE.md with bundle recovery procedures
   - Document bundle status lifecycle and failure scenarios
   - Add troubleshooting guide for common bundle failures
   - Create runbook for manual bundle recovery operations
